title: Christella dentata
taxonomy: Christella dentata is classified within the family Thelypteridaceae. It belongs to the order Polypodiales and class Polypodiopsida. This species is further classified as a member of the genus Christella and the family Thelypteridaceae. Christella dentata is a type of fern that is commonly found in tropical regions. It is a vascular plant with characteristics of a flowering plant.

intro: Christella dentata, also known as the toothed thyme fern or Christ's thyme fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which may help in reducing pain, inflammation, and the risk of certain diseases.
